steps.title: Find the slope r using two table points
steps.content:
Pick two points: $(-8, 48)$ and $(8, -96)$. Apply the slope formula:
$$r = \frac{f(x_2) - f(x_1)}{x_2 - x_1} = \frac{-96 - 48}{8 - (-8)} = \frac{-144}{16} = -9$$

steps.title: Find s using a point, then compute rs
steps.content:
Substitute point $(-8, 48)$ into $f(x) = rx + s$:
$$48 = (-9)(-8) + s$$$$48 = 72 + s$$$$s = -24$$Multiply to find $rs$:
$$rs = (-9) \times (-24) = 216$$
